output: Implemented the approved video-editor polish pass, verified it with a production build plus focused regression tests, and checkpointed progress into finalize.json.
commands_run: npx vitest run --config config/testing/vitest.config.ts src/shared/components/ui/slider.test.tsx src/app/components/LayoutMainContent.test.tsx src/shared/lib/__tests__/dragDrop.test.ts
deviations: T12: The duplicate-query warning was caused by duplicate `useQueries()` entries in `AssetPanel`, not by StrictMode or overlapping editor providers.
T1: Updated `ClipPanel` slider consumers in addition to `CompactPreview` because the shared `Slider` wrapper fix would otherwise leave two runtime-mismatched handlers inside the editor.
T13: I ran a full production build and focused Vitest coverage for touched areas, but I did not run the entire repository-wide `npm test` suite.

task_updates.executor_notes: Normalized the shared Slider wrapper to a scalar callback, updated the compact playhead consumer, and aligned the clip property sliders so the video-editor slider API is consistent at runtime. Build and focused tests passed, which catches the crash path and the wrapper contract change.
commands_run: npx vitest run --config config/testing/vitest.config.ts src/shared/components/ui/slider.test.tsx src/app/components/LayoutMainContent.test.tsx src/shared/lib/__tests__/dragDrop.test.ts

task_updates.executor_notes: Verified the installed @remotion/player package exposes acknowledgeRemotionLicense and passed it through RemotionPreview so both the compact pane and full preview stop emitting the warning. The project build completed cleanly with the prop in place.

Unused-track counting now ignores the default V1/A1 tracks and clear-unused preserves them explicitly before considering fallback empty-track retention. This matches the empty-default-track edge case the plan called out.

LayoutMainContent now only adds top padding when the editor pane is locked, so hover-open behaves as an overlay while locked mode still reserves space. Added a focused regression test for the hover-open case.
commands_run: npx vitest run --config config/testing/vitest.config.ts src/shared/components/ui/slider.test.tsx src/app/components/LayoutMainContent.test.tsx src/shared/lib/__tests__/dragDrop.test.ts

task_updates.executor_notes: Kept drag updates local to OverlayEditor: the dragged overlay now renders its own lightweight preview while an opaque mask covers the original Remotion position, so text and image overlays visually follow the cursor without re-materializing the timeline on every mousemove.

Merged track management, clear-unused, zoom, and the resize affordance into the divider strip so the standalone toolbar row is gone. The resize handler stays attached only to the center handle, which preserves divider dragging while buttons remain clickable.

Added a dedicated maximize toggle that snaps the preview to the configured minimum height and restores the prior split when toggled off. Manual divider dragging automatically exits maximize mode, so the two layout controls do not fight each other.
Left rendering disabled per the approved plan and changed the user feedback from an error toast to an informational message that explicitly frames the limitation as pending server-side work.

Added explicit timeline drag feedback for horizontal clip moves: the dragged clip now gets a brighter active state plus a projected ghost, center line, and row label that track the target slot. Existing cross-track ghost/highlight behavior remains intact.

Reused the existing skeleton placeholder flow but changed dropped external images to follow the generation pipeline: upload the image to the normal media storage path, create a generation record plus primary variant, register that generation as a timeline asset, then add the clip. Non-image files keep the existing timeline-bucket upload path.
Verified in code that PropertiesPanel already initializes assetsExpanded to false, so no repo changes were needed for this item.

The duplicate-query warning was not caused by coexisting editor providers. The actual issue was AssetPanel building useQueries entries with repeated generation IDs, which TanStack warns about. Deduplicating the query list removes the warning while still mapping each asset back to its shared query result.

Ran a full production build and targeted regression tests for the touched shared components. I did not run the repository-wide npm test suite, so verification covers the build plus focused tests rather than every existing test file.
commands_run: npx vitest run --config config/testing/vitest.config.ts src/shared/components/ui/slider.test.tsx src/app/components/LayoutMainContent.test.tsx src/shared/lib/__tests__/dragDrop.test.ts

sense_check_acknowledgments.executor_note: Verified by normalizing Slider to emit a scalar value and updating CompactPreview to consume that scalar. Build passed and the shared slider tests still pass.
Confirmed from node_modules/@remotion/player/dist/cjs/Player.d.ts that acknowledgeRemotionLicense is a valid prop, then wired it into RemotionPreview.
The counting logic now excludes DEFAULT_VIDEO_TRACKS and clear-unused keeps those IDs unconditionally, so empty V1/A1 no longer contribute to the count.
Added a focused LayoutMainContent test covering the hover-open editor pane case and it now keeps paddingTop at 0px unless the pane is locked.

Overlay drag remains local-state-only. The dragged overlay now paints its own preview while a mask covers the stale Remotion position, which resolves the visual stickiness without per-mousemove timeline commits.
Controls now live in the divider strip and the resize handler is bound only to the center handle element, so button clicks do not trigger panel resizing.
The maximize toggle switches gridTemplateRows to a minimum-height preview layout and toggling off falls back to the prior split logic; divider drags clear maximize mode.
startRender now raises an informational toast message instead of an error and keeps the render infrastructure dormant for future server-side work.
TimelineEditor now renders an explicit ghost, center line, and row label during horizontal clip drags; cross-track drag feedback continues to come from the existing ghost/highlight path.

Dropped external images now follow a generation-backed upload flow before being added to the timeline, while the existing uploading-* skeleton placeholder remains in place until the async work resolves.
Verified existing code only: PropertiesPanel initializes assetsExpanded with useState(false).
Investigated the warning path and fixed the real cause by deduplicating repeated generation-lightbox queries in AssetPanel rather than changing provider mounting behavior.
npm run build passed and the targeted vitest run passed. The full repository-wide npm test suite was not run in this execution pass.
